You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: 5c17a629).

## Issue to Resolve

Endless loop in wrapping binary expression after elvis operator
Given code below:
```
val foo =
    bar
        ?:
        throw UnsupportedOperationException("xxxxxxxxxxx")
```
and `.editorconfig`:
```
root = true

[**/*.{kt,kts}]
max_line_length = 60
ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
ktlint_experimental = enabled
ktlint_standard = enabled
```
then ktlint `0.50.0` is not able to format this in 3 consecutive runs:
```
20:32:35.107 [pool-1-thread-1] WARN com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine - Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file src/main/kotlin/Foo.kt in 3 consecutive runs of format.
```

As a line may not end with `?:` ktlint merges the last two lines. This however results in wrapping the line again to the original situation. As of that an endless loop results.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/BinaryExpressionWrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 33→
 34→/**
 35→ * Wraps a binary expression whenever the expression does not fit on the line. Wrapping a binary expression should take precedence before
 36→ * argument of function calls inside that binary expression are wrapped.
 37→ */
 38→public class BinaryExpressionWrappingRule :
 39→    StandardRule(
 40→        id = "binary-expression-wrapping",
 41→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 42→            setOf(
 43→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 44→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 45→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 46→            ),
 47→        visitorModifiers =
 48→            setOf(
 49→                RunAfterRule(ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED),
 50→            ),
 51→    ),
 52→    Rule.Experimental {
 53→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 54→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 55→
 56→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 57→        indentConfig =
 58→            IndentConfig(
 59→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 60→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 61→            )
 62→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 66→        node: ASTNode,
 67→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        when (node.elementType) {
 71→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun visitExpression(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→    ) {
 80→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 81→
 82→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 83→        node
 84→            .takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType.anyOf(PROPERTY, FUN) }
 85→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 86→                binaryExpression
 87→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 88→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 89→                    ?: false
 90→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 91→            ?.let { expression ->
 92→                emit(
 93→                    expression.startOffset,
 94→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 95→                    true,
 96→                )
 97→                if (autoCorrect) {
 98→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun visitOperationReference(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
111→    ) {
112→        node
113→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
114→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
115→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
116→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
117→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
118→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
119→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
120→            ?.let { operationReference ->
121→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() || cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference)) {
122→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
123→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
124→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
125→                } else {
126→                    operationReference
127→                        .nextSibling()
128→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
129→                            emit(
130→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
131→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
132→                                true,
133→                            )
134→                            if (autoCorrect) {
135→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
136→                            }
137→                        }
138→                }
139→            }
140→    }
141→
142→    private fun ASTNode.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() =
143→        parent { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
144→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION }
145→            ?.nextCodeSibling()
146→            .let { it?.elementType == LAMBDA_ARGUMENT }
147→
148→    private fun cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference: ASTNode) =
149→        operationReference
150→            .takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
151→            ?.let {
152→                val stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf = operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
153→                maxLineLength <=
154→                    it
155→                        .leavesOnLine()
156→                        .takeWhile { leaf -> leaf != stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf }
157→                        .lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix()
158→            }
159→            ?: false
160→
161→    private fun ASTNode.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() = leavesOnLine().lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() > maxLineLength
162→
163→    private fun Sequence<ASTNode>.lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() =
164→        joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
165→            .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
166→            .length
167→}
168→
169→private fun IElementType.anyOf(vararg elementType: IElementType): Boolean = elementType.contains(this)
170→

Now I have a clear understanding. Let me implement the fix. The key insight is:

When the operation reference is ELVIS (`?:`), wrapping after it puts `?:` at end of line. Chain-wrapping will then move `?:` to the beginning of the next line, joining the RHS. If the resulting line (`indent + "?: " + rhs_text`) still exceeds max line length, wrapping is futile and creates an infinite loop. In this case, we should emit a non-auto-correctable violation.

Now I understand the structure. In a BINARY_EXPRESSION, the children are:
1. Left-hand side expression (e.g., `bar`)
2. Whitespace
3. OPERATION_REFERENCE (containing ELVIS `?:`)

So `childIndentOf(node)` = `node.indent().plus(indent)`. That's `\n<parent indent><indent>`.

For the ELVIS node in our case:
- The ELVIS node is inside the OPERATION_REFERENCE, which is inside the BINARY_EXPRESSION
- `node.indent()` for the ELVIS `?:` - looks backwards for the first whitespace with newline

OK, let me think about this differently. The chain-wrapping rule operates on the ELVIS leaf node directly. After `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` wraps, we'd have:

```
    bar ?:
        throw UnsupportedOperationException(...)
```

The ELVIS leaf's `indent()` would look backward and find the newline before `bar`, giving an indent of `    ` (4 spaces). Then `childIndentOf(elvis)` = `\n    ` + `    ` = `\n        `. So chain-wrapping would put:

```
    bar
        ?: throw UnsupportedOperationException(...)
```

The line length of this = 8 (indent) + 3 (`?: `) + length of `throw UnsupportedOperationException("xxxxxxxxxxx")` = 8 + 3 + 52 = 63

With max_line_length = 60, this exceeds it, creating the loop.

Now for the fix. In the `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule`, when the operation is ELVIS, I need to check: would the line AFTER chain-wrapping correction still exceed max line length? If so, don't auto-correct.

The approach: check if the operation reference's first child is ELVIS. If so, compute the would-be line length after chain-wrapping: the indent of the operator + one more indent + `?: ` + the right-hand side text. If that exceeds max line length, emit as non-auto-correctable.

Let me understand this test case. Looking at the test:

```
// $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER        $EOL_CHAR
val foo1 = foo() ?: "fooooooooooo" +
        "bar"
```

The MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER + spaces + EOL_CHAR establishes a max line length. Let me count characters. The marker comment line defines the max line length.

For `foo1`: the operation reference is `?:` (ELVIS). The next code sibling would be `"fooooooooooo" + "bar"` (which is a binary expression). 

Wait, but `cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference` already has:
```kotlin
.takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
```

So if the next code sibling IS a binary expression, `cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference` returns false (doesn't apply). My new function `wrappingOfElvisOperatorCausesInfiniteLoop` might be catching this case when it shouldn't.

Let me trace through for `foo1`:
- `val foo1 = foo() ?: "fooooooooooo" + "bar"`
- The outer binary expression is `foo() ?: "fooooooooooo" + "bar"`
- The operation reference is `?:`
- The next code sibling of `?:` is `"fooooooooooo" + "bar"` (a BINARY_EXPRESSION)

For the `?:` operation reference:
- `firstChildNode.elementType == ELVIS` → true
- `nextCodeSibling()` = `"fooooooooooo" + "bar"` (text length includes all the text of the RHS)
- `rightHandSideExpression.textLength` = length of `"fooooooooooo" + "bar"` without whitespace

Wait, but `textLength` on the binary expression node includes all text including internal whitespace. Let me think...

Actually, the issue is that for the binary expression `"fooooooooooo" + "bar"`, the textLength would be the total text including the internal newline and spaces (since `"bar"` is on the next line). That makes my calculation wrong for this case.

So `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER` = `"Max line length marker:"` (23 chars), and `EOL_CHAR` = `'#'`. The max line length is the index of `#` + 1.

For the test:
```
// $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER        $EOL_CHAR
```
This becomes:
```
// Max line length marker:        #
```
Index of `#` = 34 (0-based), so max_line_length = 35.

Now the code line:
```
val foo1 = foo() ?: "fooooooooooo" +
```
= `val foo1 = foo() ?: "fooooooooooo" +` = 38 chars

And my function checks the `?:` operator reference. Let me see:
- `operationReference.firstChildNode.elementType == ELVIS` → true
- `indent` of `operationReference` → looking backward from `?:` finds the newline before `val`, so indent is `""` (no indent, it's at top level)
- `indentLength` = 0 + 4 (indent config) = 4 
- `rightHandSideExpression` = next code sibling after `?:` which is `"fooooooooooo" + "bar"`
- `rightHandSideExpression.textLength` = the text of this binary expression including internal whitespace

Hmm, the `textLength` of a multi-line node would include newlines. That would make the line length calculation wrong. Let me think about this differently.

Actually, for the issue at hand (the specific elvis infinite loop bug), the RHS is a single-line expression like `throw UnsupportedOperationException("xxxxxxxxxxx")`. For multi-line RHS like `"fooooooooooo" + "bar"` which spans multiple lines, the text length would include the newline, making my calculation too large, and it would incorrectly mark it as non-auto-correctable.

The problem is that `textLength` includes all text including newlines in child nodes. I need to only consider the FIRST LINE of the RHS.

But wait - the key question is what happens after chain-wrapping. When binary-expression-wrapping wraps at `?:`, the line becomes:
```
val foo1 = foo() ?:
    "fooooooooooo" + "bar"
```

Then chain-wrapping moves `?:` to start of next line:
```
val foo1 = foo()
    ?: "fooooooooooo" + "bar"
```

The resulting line = `    ?: "fooooooooooo" + "bar"` = 4 + 3 + the first line of the RHS. But the RHS in this case is `"fooooooooooo" + \n        "bar"`. After chain-wrapping joins `?:` with the next line (space after `?:` then the RHS starts), the first line would be `    ?: "fooooooooooo" +`.

Hmm, this is getting complicated with multi-line RHS. Let me look at this from a different angle.

The infinite loop happens specifically when:
1. The operator is ELVIS
2. Wrapping puts `?:` at end of line
3. Chain-wrapping moves `?:` to beginning of next line, joining it with RHS
4. The resulting first line still exceeds max line length
5. Go to step 2

For the infinite loop to happen, the critical thing is that the RHS after the `?: ` on the same line still makes it too long. But with a multi-line RHS, only the first part of it ends up on the same line as `?:`.

Actually wait, let me reconsider what chain-wrapping does. Looking at the chain-wrapping code again:

```kotlin
if (node.elementType == ELVIS) {
    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
}
```

It inserts a newline + indent before `?:` and a space after `?:`. So the `?:` gets moved to a new line. The next sibling (whitespace + RHS) stays where it is.

Wait, `upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")` replaces the whitespace after `?:` with just a space. So after chain-wrapping:
- Before `?:`: newline + indent
- After `?:`: single space
- Then the RHS text starts

So the result for the multi-line case would be:
```
val foo1 = foo()
    ?: "fooooooooooo" +
        "bar"
```

Actually no, the RHS is a BINARY_EXPRESSION node: `"fooooooooooo" + "bar"`. The whitespace between them is inside that node. The `?:` is inside the OPERATION_REFERENCE. The chain-wrapping rule operates on the ELVIS leaf node itself, which is inside the OPERATION_REFERENCE.

Let me re-examine the tree structure. The outer binary expression:
```
BINARY_EXPRESSION
  ├─ left: CALL_EXPRESSION (foo())
  ├─ WHITE_SPACE
  ├─ OPERATION_REFERENCE
  │   └─ ELVIS (?:)
  ├─ WHITE_SPACE
  └─ right: BINARY_EXPRESSION ("fooooooooooo" + "bar")
       ├─ STRING ("fooooooooooo")
       ├─ WHITE_SPACE
       ├─ OPERATION_REFERENCE
       │   └─ PLUS (+)
       ├─ WHITE_SPACE (\n        )
       └─ STRING ("bar")
```

So when BinaryExpressionWrappingRule wraps at the `?:`, it replaces the WHITE_SPACE after OPERATION_REFERENCE with `\n    ` (indent). Result:
```
val foo1 = foo() ?:
    "fooooooooooo" +
        "bar"
```

Then chain-wrapping sees the ELVIS node with a newline after it. It calls:
```kotlin
node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
```

The `upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe` modifies the whitespace BEFORE the ELVIS. But ELVIS is a child of OPERATION_REFERENCE. The whitespace before OPERATION_REFERENCE in the binary expression is between `foo()` and `?:`. So it becomes `\n    ` before the OPERATION_REFERENCE.

The `upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")` sets the whitespace after ELVIS to `" "`. But ELVIS is the only child of OPERATION_REFERENCE. The whitespace after OPERATION_REFERENCE (which is between OPERATION_REFERENCE and the RHS) was just changed to `\n    ` by binary-expression-wrapping. Now chain-wrapping changes it to `" "`.

Result:
```
val foo1 = foo()
    ?: "fooooooooooo" +
        "bar"
```

Line: `    ?: "fooooooooooo" +` = 4 + 3 + 16 = 23 characters. That's under 35 (max line length for this test).

So my function should NOT flag this case. But my current implementation uses `rightHandSideExpression.textLength` which includes the full multi-line text. Let me fix this.

I need to compute the length of just the first line of the RHS. I can use `leavesOnLine()` or compute based on the text up to the first newline.

Actually, a simpler approach: I should compute the length as `indent + "?: " + first_line_of_RHS`. The first line of the RHS is from the start of the RHS text up to the first newline (or end of text).

114→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }

119→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null

129→                } else {

131→                        .nextSibling()
132→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
133→                            emit(
134→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
135→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
136→                                true,
137→                            )
138→                            if (autoCorrect) {
139→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
140→                            }

142→                }

146→    private fun wrappingOfElvisOperatorCausesInfiniteLoop(operationReference: ASTNode): Boolean {

158→        // multiple lines.
159→        val rightHandSideFirstLineLength = rightHandSideExpression.text.substringBefore('\n').length

The fix is clean and minimal. Let me summarize the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### Root Cause
The `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` and `ChainWrappingRule` create an infinite loop when dealing with ELVIS (`?:`) operators in binary expressions that exceed max line length:

1. **BinaryExpressionWrappingRule** wraps after `?:`, placing `?:` at the end of the line
2. **ChainWrappingRule** says a line must not end with `?:`, so it moves `?:` to the beginning of the next line, joining it with the right-hand side
3. The joined line still exceeds max line length, so step 1 triggers again → infinite loop

### Fix
Added a new check `wrappingOfElvisOperatorCausesInfiniteLoop()` in `BinaryExpressionWrappingRule` that detects when:
- The operation reference is an ELVIS operator (`?:`)
- After wrapping AND subsequent chain-wrapping correction, the resulting line (`<indent>?: <right-hand-side>`) would still exceed max line length

When this condition is detected, the violation is emitted as **non-auto-correctable** (`canBeAutoCorrected = false`) instead of attempting to wrap (which would cause the infinite loop).

### Changes
- **`BinaryExpressionWrappingRule.kt`**: 
  - Added import for `ELVIS` element type
  - Added `wrappingOfElvisOperatorCausesInfiniteLoop()` function
  - Added the function as a condition check in `visitOperationReference()` alongside existing checks

 43→public class ChainWrappingRule :

 53→    private val prefixTokens = TokenSet.create(PLUS, MINUS)
 54→    private val nextLineTokens = TokenSet.create(DOT, SAFE_ACCESS, ELVIS)
 55→    private val noSpaceAroundTokens = TokenSet.create(DOT, SAFE_ACCESS)

 58→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {

 64→    }

 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        /*
 72→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement (DOT) | "."
 73→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl (WHITE_SPACE) | "\n        "
 74→           org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtCallExpression (CALL_EXPRESSION)
 75→         */
 76→        val elementType = node.elementType
 77→        if (nextLineTokens.contains(elementType)) {
 78→            if (node.isPartOfComment()) {
 79→                return
 80→            }
 81→            val nextLeaf = node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf()
 82→            if (nextLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && !node.isElvisOperatorAndComment()) {
 83→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line must not end with \"${node.text}\"", true)
 84→                if (autoCorrect) {
 85→                    // rewriting
 86→                    // <prevLeaf><node="."><nextLeaf="\n"> to
 87→                    // <prevLeaf><delete space if any><nextLeaf="\n"><node="."><space if needed>
 88→                    // (or)
 89→                    // <prevLeaf><node="."><spaceBeforeComment><comment><nextLeaf="\n"> to
 90→                    // <prevLeaf><delete space if any><spaceBeforeComment><comment><nextLeaf="\n"><node="."><space if needed>
 91→                    if (node.elementType == ELVIS) {
 92→                        node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
 93→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 94→                    } else {
 95→                        node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
 96→                        (nextLeaf as LeafElement).rawInsertAfterMe(node as LeafElement)
 97→                    }
 98→                }
 99→            }
100→        } else if (sameLineTokens.contains(elementType) || prefixTokens.contains(elementType)) {
101→            if (node.isPartOfComment()) {
102→                return
103→            }
104→            val prevLeaf = node.prevLeaf()
105→            if (
106→                prevLeaf?.elementType == WHITE_SPACE &&
107→                prevLeaf.textContains('\n') &&
108→                // fn(*typedArray<...>()) case
109→                (elementType != MUL || !prevLeaf.isPartOfSpread()) &&
110→                // unary +/-
111→                (!prefixTokens.contains(elementType) || !node.isInPrefixPosition())
112→            ) {
113→                emit(node.startOffset, "Line must not begin with \"${node.text}\"", true)
114→                if (autoCorrect) {
115→                    // rewriting
116→                    // <insertionPoint><prevLeaf="\n"><node="&&"><nextLeaf=" "> to
117→                    // <insertionPoint><prevLeaf=" "><node="&&"><nextLeaf="\n"><delete node="&&"><delete nextLeaf=" ">
118→                    // (or)
119→                    // <insertionPoint><spaceBeforeComment><comment><prevLeaf="\n"><node="&&"><nextLeaf=" "> to
120→                    // <insertionPoint><space if needed><node="&&"><spaceBeforeComment><comment><prevLeaf="\n"><delete node="&&"><delete nextLeaf=" ">
121→                    val nextLeaf = node.nextLeaf()
122→                    if (nextLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
123→                        nextLeaf.node.treeParent.removeChild(nextLeaf.node)
124→                    }
125→                    val insertionPoint = prevLeaf.prevCodeLeaf() as LeafPsiElement
126→                    (node as LeafPsiElement).treeParent.removeChild(node)
127→                    insertionPoint.rawInsertAfterMe(node)
128→                    (insertionPoint as ASTNode).upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
129→                }
130→            }

132→    }

134→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfSpread() =
135→        prevCodeLeaf()?.let { leaf ->
136→            val type = leaf.elementType
137→            type == LPAR ||
138→                type == COMMA ||
139→                type == LBRACE ||
140→                type == ELSE_KEYWORD ||
141→                KtTokens.OPERATIONS.contains(type)
142→        } == true
143→
144→    private fun ASTNode.isInPrefixPosition() = treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == PREFIX_EXPRESSION

146→    private fun ASTNode.isElvisOperatorAndComment(): Boolean {
147→        return elementType == ELVIS &&
148→            leaves().takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() || it.isPartOfComment() }.any()

 34→/**
 35→ * Wraps a binary expression whenever the expression does not fit on the line. Wrapping a binary expression should take precedence before
 36→ * argument of function calls inside that binary expression are wrapped.
 37→ */
 38→public class BinaryExpressionWrappingRule :
 39→    StandardRule(
 40→        id = "binary-expression-wrapping",
 41→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 42→            setOf(
 43→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 44→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 45→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 46→            ),
 47→        visitorModifiers =
 48→            setOf(
 49→                RunAfterRule(ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED),
 50→            ),
 51→    ),
 52→    Rule.Experimental {
 53→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 54→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 55→
 56→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 57→        indentConfig =
 58→            IndentConfig(
 59→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 60→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 61→            )
 62→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 66→        node: ASTNode,
 67→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        when (node.elementType) {
 71→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun visitExpression(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→    ) {
 80→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 81→
 82→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 83→        node
 84→            .takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType.anyOf(PROPERTY, FUN) }
 85→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 86→                binaryExpression
 87→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 88→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 89→                    ?: false
 90→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 91→            ?.let { expression ->
 92→                emit(
 93→                    expression.startOffset,
 94→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 95→                    true,
 96→                )
 97→                if (autoCorrect) {
 98→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun visitOperationReference(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
110→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
111→    ) {
112→        node
113→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
114→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
115→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
116→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
117→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
118→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
119→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
120→            ?.let { operationReference ->
121→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() || cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference)) {
122→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
123→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
124→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
125→                } else {
126→                    operationReference
127→                        .nextSibling()
128→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
129→                            emit(
130→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
131→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
132→                                true,
133→                            )
134→                            if (autoCorrect) {
135→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
136→                            }
137→                        }
138→                }
139→            }
140→    }
141→
142→    private fun ASTNode.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() =
143→        parent { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
144→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION }
145→            ?.nextCodeSibling()
146→            .let { it?.elementType == LAMBDA_ARGUMENT }
147→
148→    private fun cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference: ASTNode) =
149→        operationReference
150→            .takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
151→            ?.let {
152→                val stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf = operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
153→                maxLineLength <=
154→                    it
155→                        .leavesOnLine()
156→                        .takeWhile { leaf -> leaf != stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf }
157→                        .lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix()
158→            }
159→            ?: false
160→
161→    private fun ASTNode.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() = leavesOnLine().lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() > maxLineLength
162→

164→        joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
165→            .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
166→            .length
167→}

 37→ * argument of function calls inside that binary expression are wrapped.

 40→    StandardRule(
 41→        id = "binary-expression-wrapping",
 42→        usesEditorConfigProperties =

 45→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 46→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 47→            ),
 48→        visitorModifiers =
 49→            setOf(
 50→                RunAfterRule(ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED),
 51→            ),
 52→    ),

 54→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 55→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 56→
 57→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 58→        indentConfig =
 59→            IndentConfig(
 60→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 61→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 62→            )
 63→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        when (node.elementType) {
 72→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 73→        }
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    private fun visitExpression(
 77→        node: ASTNode,
 78→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 79→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 80→    ) {
 81→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 82→
 83→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 84→        node
 85→            .takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType.anyOf(PROPERTY, FUN) }
 86→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 87→                binaryExpression
 88→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 89→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 90→                    ?: false
 91→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 92→            ?.let { expression ->
 93→                emit(

 95→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 96→                    true,
 97→                )
 98→                if (autoCorrect) {
 99→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
100→                }
101→            }

104→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
105→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
106→    }

108→    private fun visitOperationReference(
109→        node: ASTNode,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
112→    ) {
113→        node
114→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
115→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
116→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
117→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
118→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
119→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
120→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
121→            ?.let { operationReference ->
122→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() ||
123→                    cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference) ||
124→                    wrappingOfElvisOperatorCausesInfiniteLoop(operationReference)
125→                ) {
126→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
127→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
128→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
129→                } else {
130→                    operationReference
131→                        .nextSibling()
132→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
133→                            emit(
134→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
135→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
136→                                true,
137→                            )
138→                            if (autoCorrect) {
139→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
140→                            }
141→                        }
142→                }
143→            }

146→    private fun wrappingOfElvisOperatorCausesInfiniteLoop(operationReference: ASTNode): Boolean {
147→        if (operationReference.firstChildNode?.elementType != ELVIS) {
148→            return false
149→        }
150→        // When the binary expression is wrapped at the elvis operator, the chain-wrapping rule will move the elvis operator
151→        // to the beginning of the next line (as a line may not end with an elvis operator). If the resulting line still
152→        // exceeds the max line length, wrapping and chain-wrapping will keep undoing each other, causing an infinite loop.
153→        val indent = operationReference.indent(includeNewline = false)
154→        val indentLength = indent.length + indentConfig.indent.length
155→        val rightHandSideExpression = operationReference.nextCodeSibling() ?: return false
156→        // After chain-wrapping, the line will be: <indent><extra-indent>?: <right-hand-side>
157→        val lineLength = indentLength + "?: ".length + rightHandSideExpression.textLength
158→        return lineLength > maxLineLength

168→        operationReference

170→            ?.let {
171→                val stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf = operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
172→                maxLineLength <=
173→                    it
174→                        .leavesOnLine()
175→                        .takeWhile { leaf -> leaf != stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf }
176→                        .lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix()
177→            }
178→            ?: false

183→        joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
184→            .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
185→            .length

 32→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 33→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 34→
 35→/**
 36→ * Wraps a binary expression whenever the expression does not fit on the line. Wrapping a binary expression should take precedence before
 37→ * argument of function calls inside that binary expression are wrapped.
 38→ */
 39→public class BinaryExpressionWrappingRule :
 40→    StandardRule(
 41→        id = "binary-expression-wrapping",
 42→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 43→            setOf(
 44→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 45→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 46→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 47→            ),
 48→        visitorModifiers =
 49→            setOf(
 50→                RunAfterRule(ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED),
 51→            ),
 52→    ),
 53→    Rule.Experimental {
 54→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 55→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 56→
 57→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 58→        indentConfig =
 59→            IndentConfig(
 60→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 61→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 62→            )
 63→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        when (node.elementType) {
 72→            BINARY_EXPRESSION -> visitExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 73→        }
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    private fun visitExpression(
 77→        node: ASTNode,
 78→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 79→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 80→    ) {
 81→        require(node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION)
 82→
 83→        // First check whether the entire expression has to be pushed to the next line after and assignment in a property or function
 84→        node
 85→            .takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType.anyOf(PROPERTY, FUN) }
 86→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
 87→                binaryExpression
 88→                    .prevSibling { it.elementType == EQ }
 89→                    ?.let { noNewLineInClosedRange(it, binaryExpression.firstChildLeafOrSelf()) }
 90→                    ?: false
 91→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
 92→            ?.let { expression ->
 93→                emit(
 94→                    expression.startOffset,
 95→                    "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line between assignment and expression",
 96→                    true,
 97→                )
 98→                if (autoCorrect) {
 99→                    expression.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expression.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
100→                }
101→            }
102→
103→        node
104→            .findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)
105→            ?.let { operationReference -> visitOperationReference(operationReference, emit, autoCorrect) }
106→    }
107→
108→    private fun visitOperationReference(
109→        node: ASTNode,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
112→    ) {
113→        node
114→            .takeIf { it.elementType == OPERATION_REFERENCE }
115→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
116→            ?.takeIf { binaryExpression ->
117→                // Ignore binary expression inside raw string literals. Raw string literals are allowed to exceed max-line-length. Wrapping
118→                // (each) binary expression inside such a literal seems to create more chaos than it resolves.
119→                binaryExpression.parent { it.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY } == null
120→            }?.takeIf { it.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() }
121→            ?.let { operationReference ->
122→                if (node.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() ||
123→                    cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference) ||
124→                    wrappingOfElvisOperatorCausesInfiniteLoop(operationReference)
125→                ) {
126→                    // Wrapping after operation reference might not be the best place in case of a call expression or just won't work as
127→                    // the left hand side still does not fit on a single line
128→                    emit(operationReference.startOffset, "Line is exceeding max line length", false)
129→                } else {
130→                    operationReference
131→                        .nextSibling()
132→                        ?.let { nextSibling ->
133→                            emit(
134→                                nextSibling.startOffset,
135→                                "Line is exceeding max line length. Break line after operator in binary expression",
136→                                true,
137→                            )
138→                            if (autoCorrect) {
139→                                nextSibling.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(operationReference.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent))
140→                            }
141→                        }
142→                }
143→            }
144→    }
145→
146→    private fun wrappingOfElvisOperatorCausesInfiniteLoop(operationReference: ASTNode): Boolean {
147→        if (operationReference.firstChildNode?.elementType != ELVIS) {
148→            return false
149→        }
150→        // When the binary expression is wrapped at the elvis operator, the chain-wrapping rule will move the elvis operator
151→        // to the beginning of the next line (as a line may not end with an elvis operator). If the resulting line still
152→        // exceeds the max line length, wrapping and chain-wrapping will keep undoing each other, causing an infinite loop.
153→        val indent = operationReference.indent(includeNewline = false)
154→        val indentLength = indent.length + indentConfig.indent.length
155→        val rightHandSideExpression = operationReference.nextCodeSibling() ?: return false
156→        // After chain-wrapping, the line will be: <indent><extra-indent>?: <right-hand-side-first-line>
157→        // Only consider the first line of the right-hand side expression as multi-line expressions would be split across
158→        // multiple lines.
159→        val rightHandSideFirstLineLength = rightHandSideExpression.text.substringBefore('\n').length
160→        val lineLength = indentLength + "?: ".length + rightHandSideFirstLineLength
161→        return lineLength > maxLineLength
162→    }
163→
164→    private fun ASTNode.isCallExpressionFollowedByLambdaArgument() =
165→        parent { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
166→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION }
167→            ?.nextCodeSibling()
168→            .let { it?.elementType == LAMBDA_ARGUMENT }
169→
170→    private fun cannotBeWrappedAtOperationReference(operationReference: ASTNode) =
171→        operationReference
172→            .takeUnless { it.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION }
173→            ?.let {
174→                val stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf = operationReference.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
175→                maxLineLength <=
176→                    it
177→                        .leavesOnLine()
178→                        .takeWhile { leaf -> leaf != stopAtOperationReferenceLeaf }
179→                        .lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix()
180→            }
181→            ?: false
182→
183→    private fun ASTNode.isOnLineExceedingMaxLineLength() = leavesOnLine().lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() > maxLineLength
184→
185→    private fun Sequence<ASTNode>.lengthWithoutNewlinePrefix() =
186→        joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
187→            .dropWhile { it == '\n' }
188→            .length
189→}
190→
191→private fun IElementType.anyOf(vararg elementType: IElementType): Boolean = elementType.contains(this)
192→
193→public val BINARY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = BinaryExpressionWrappingRule().ruleId
